_meta.purpose: Public, published quality-measure / accreditation time-standards the compliance lane checks captured events against. The 'absent-by-deadline' arm of the extract-and-compare engine fires a miss/possible-miss when clock elapses past target with no matching captured event.
_meta.status: DEMO REFERENCE — compiled from public published standards. Pending clinician sign-off (Damian Mosher, ER MD). NOT for clinical use as-is.

measures.name: Trauma surgeon at bedside (highest-level activation)
measures.reimbursement_link: Supports the trauma team activation charge (rev code 0681 / G0390) when activation criteria are met AND documented AND the team responded.

measures.name: Highest-level trauma activation criteria (any one qualifies)

mechanism: auto vs. pedestrian/bike thrown or > 20 mph

measures.reimbursement_link: Meeting ANY criterion + documented activation qualifies the trauma-activation fee. This is the rule-check the reimbursement lane runs — tractable, not full DRG abstraction.

measures.name: Definitive airway placement confirmed
measures.reimbursement_link: Endotracheal intubation charge (CPT 31500) requires documented confirmation.

measures.name: EFAST completed during primary/secondary survey
measures.standard: DEMO EXPECTATION — not a regulatory clock. Reasonable early-survey window used to demo proactive miss-detection.
measures.provenance: NONE — THIS NUMBER IS OURS. We are not aware of any ACS/TQIP/CMS numeric FAST target; ATLS places FAST as an adjunct to the primary survey, which is a phase, not a clock. Asked as CLIN-Q7, unanswered. NEVER call this a standard out loud. If challenged: 'a configurable expectation, not a regulatory clock — the mechanism is the point, not the number.'
measures.demo_note: SCRIPT 1 (the live demo, ~3:45) performs FAST at 1:50 — this arm is SATISFIED and never fires, so nothing on stage rests on this number. Only SCRIPT 2's T+10:00 pop does. The live proactive beat is the vitals-freshness arm, which needs no external standard at all.

measures.name: Critical care time (first 30-74 min)
measures.reimbursement_link: Documented critical care time >= 30 min supports CC-TIME (99291).
